methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Charm Industrial is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Peter Reinhardt that has raised $100M+. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 companies: one public entity (LNZA) and eight private peers, including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc., all at the same stage.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression valuation, using $100M total raised as a base proxy and applying a 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, implies $190M. Peer-median valuation across six same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median of $355M, though the peer set exhibits material dispersion (range: $120M to $5.0B), with Commonwealth Fusion Systems at the high end at $5.0B and the lower quartile near $300M.

Synthesizing these methods produces a triangulated valuation range of $162M (low), $272M (median), and $408M (high). The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound, while peer multiples establish the ceiling. Key uncertainty stems from the wide dispersion in the peer set—a 40x spread between minimum and maximum—reflecting heterogeneity in technology maturity, capital efficiency, and market addressability within the climate-tech sector at Series B. Charm Industrial's specific technology, unit economics, and market position relative to peers would materially inform positioning within this range.
